title: Open Standards-Traceable Digital Twin for Chemical Integrity Management in Oil and Gas, CCS and Hydrogen Systems
novelty_statement: We present an open-source, standards-traceable digital twin for chemical integrity management that couples (a) Davies activity-coefficient electrolyte scale prediction, (b) mechanistic CO2 corrosion combining NORSOK M-506 with a Nesic Sherwood mass-transfer correlation and a temperature-dependent Langmuir inhibitor isotherm, (c) closed-loop deposition coupling with Beggs-and-Brills hydraulics, (d) 1-D plug-flow H2S scavenger breakthrough, and (e) Bayesian root-cause inference, all exposed via the Model Context Protocol so that AI agents can drive integrity workflows. Openly available frameworks typically address only a subset of these capability areas and rarely expose an agent-ready, machine-readable interface; the present framework is open, traceable to source standards, fully reproducible (47 JUnit regression tests), and extensible to plant historian data (PI, IP.21) and the OSDU subsurface data platform.

research_questions.question: Can a single open-source framework reproduce industry benchmark results for the four dominant chemical integrity threats?
research_questions.hypothesis: Yes — Davies SI for CaCO3/BaSO4/CaSO4/SrSO4 reproduces NACE TM0374 reference brines within 0.2 SI units; NORSOK M-506 + Nesic mass-transfer reproduces the M-506 Annex B benchmark within 15 percent; Langmuir kinetics reproduce published inhibitor dose-response curves; packed-bed scavenger breakthrough matches the reactive-transport analytic solution within 5 percent.
research_questions.method: Run 47 JUnit regression tests covering each routine; cross-check published examples.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: All 47 tests pass; benchmark deviations within stated bounds.

research_questions.question: Does coupling deposition with hydraulics change the predicted time-to-blockage versus an uncoupled calculation?
research_questions.hypothesis: Yes — closed-loop coupling raises predicted velocities and shortens predicted time to a 5-percent restriction threshold for high-supersaturation brines.
research_questions.method: Run ClosedLoopDepositionSolver vs single-pass ScaleDepositionAccumulator for an anonymised tieback case.

research_questions.question: Can AI agents drive integrity workflows reliably through the Model Context Protocol?
research_questions.hypothesis: Yes — the runChemistry MCP tool exposes JSON-in / JSON-out access to every chemistry routine with structured error envelopes that agents can act on.
research_questions.method: Demonstrate four MCP analyses (electrolyteScale, mechanisticCorrosion, langmuirInhibitor, packedBedScavenger).
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: All four analyses return well-formed JSON with status, data and provenance fields.

research_questions.question: Is the framework extensible to plant historian and subsurface data platforms used by operating companies?
research_questions.hypothesis: Yes — the existing tagreader integration (PI / IP.21) and the OSDU well/reservoir API can be wired into the chemistry runners through the same JSON contract used by MCP.
research_questions.method: Discuss architecture and provide future-work integration sketches.

sections: Mechanistic CO2 Corrosion with Inhibitor Coupling
Model Context Protocol Integration for AI Agents
Case Study: Subsea Tieback Digital Twin
Future Work: Plant Historian and OSDU Integration
